Hey guys, welcome back to lesson four of the first module. So this is just going to be a very quick overarching view of those five core subsequences I alluded to earlier, and these are the ones that are really going to go, that we're really going to go deeper into through modules three through seven. So for now, I just want to give you a big overarching glance at it, and then we're going to dive in deeper throughout the rest of the course. I also want to remind you at this point that, you know, while launch strategies and product funnels and systems are always going to be evolving and changing, what you're going to be learning here is designed to complement and play nicely with whichever method you're using. So whether that's going to be a webinar or live or evergreen, whether it's going to be a three part pre-recorded video series or workshops or a seven day challenge, whatever you're doing, whatever style of launch you are going to be executing for your funnel. This system is really meant to just show you those microconversions that need to take place across the journey and the types of emails and sales messaging we use to create and facilitate that narrative. So with all that said, here is your first look at the full coaching, the conversion framework. And as you can see, it's really all about moving your prospect from point A to point fire, as well as those major milestones that we have to hit along the way to really get them there. And we're going to look at those in greater detail in the next lesson as well. So in addition to the milestones, we also have those five major sub sequences that are designed to accomplish it. So we have the segmentation, the confirmation, the pre-launch, the launch, the sales, and the closing. So I know that sounds like there's six, but I actually like to tie the segmentation and confirmation together because they kind of work together as a whole. So those are going to be the basis for the rest of the course. In module three, we're going to dive deeper into the segmentation. We're going to take a look in confirmation as well, which is probably the most under-leveraged and underutilized email in your entire campaign because if you do that one right, that's where you're setting up that consistency loop. And we're going to talk about consistency loops as well. The pre-launch is where you're going to have that opportunity to really re-shift and reframe those beliefs in that moment of least friction. You know, your product hasn't been offered yet, so it's a lot easier and smoother to get your prospect to buy in, to new, more empowering ideas that later on your product is going to support. The launch simply refers to the moment your Courser program first starts accepting enrollments. And there's a huge mistake that so many program creators, course creators make at this moment that just scare off potential customers. So we're going to address that full on and show you what this sequence really is coaching once we get into module five. In module six, we're going to be talking about the sales emails. And you probably noticed right off the bat, there's a distinction we're making between the sales emails and the closing emails. Most people just loop those in together as general follow-ups, but you're going to learn why we separate them and some of the things that you could get away with in the closing that you definitely want to avoid doing in the sales. Some things that are actually pretty, pretty counterproductive actually have the reverse opposite effect, and it all has to do with where you place those messages and what you're coaching at the right time. So we're going to look at those. And then of course, we're going to cap it off with the closing sequence. This is going to be the last 48 hours of your launch where you'll typically really ramp up your efforts, you know, be in their inbox, maybe two to four times a day, even, and really just hitting them with the messages that really not just convey urgency. That's just one step. You're going to see what's actually at play here. What we're actually coaching urgency is just one small part of it. And unless you're really tackling all those other elements that your prospect needs to see at this point, at this point, right before they become a buyer, then chances are we've been leaving some sales on the table. So those are the five sub sequences at a glance. And for now, I just want to leave you with the takeaway that, you know, each of those sub sequences has its own role in moving the prospect across that buyer's journey. Each one is really selling or coaching its own set of precursors to accomplish its underlying milestone. And as we go deeper into the course, you'll also get a series of email, theme plates and guiding questions to show you exactly how to activate and achieve those micro conversions. So that's all you need to get started. I really want to keep you laser focused on building out these core sub sequences before getting too fancy with other types of sub sequences that could be initiated through behavior triggers or automation. You know, we could sometimes really fall down that rabbit hole and there is a time and place to do it. And it is really exciting with the tools we have available to us, but I really want to encourage you to just dial in these five sub sequences. That's what this course is going to be dedicated to. And of course, once that's up and running, you will get the bonus on contextology that will show you some of the other powerful little profit minds you can create within your funnel. So that's it for now. Thank you for being along for the ride in the next module. We are going to dive into direct response 2.0, which is going to be all about how we extract the content that goes into this funnel. So take a little breather and I'll catch you in the next lesson.
